properties.name: Sun Trap entrance
properties.text: A sheltered, sunny corner near the northern edge of the park — a quiet spot to pause before heading into the woodland and gardens.

properties.name: Lord Holland Memorial
properties.text: A seated bronze statue of Henry Vassall-Fox, 3rd Baron Holland, begun by G. F. Watts and completed by Joseph Edgar Boehm, unveiled in 1872.

properties.name: The Oak Throne
properties.text: A carved seat among the old oaks of Holland Park, one of several wooden sculptures dotted through the woodland.

properties.name: Kyoto Garden
properties.text: A Japanese garden given by the Chamber of Commerce of Kyoto and opened in 1991 to mark the Japan Festival, with a tiered waterfall, koi pond and resident peacocks.

properties.name: Fukushima Garden
properties.text: Opened in 2012 as a gesture of thanks for British support following the 2011 earthquake and tsunami, this calm garden complements the neighbouring Kyoto Garden.

properties.name: Chess Playground
properties.text: An outdoor play and games area in the park, a relaxed place for families and visitors.

properties.name: The Belvedere
properties.text: A restaurant housed in the former summer ballroom of Holland House, overlooking the formal gardens.

properties.name: Arcade of Orangery
properties.text: The arcaded Orangery, once part of the Holland House estate, is now used as an events and exhibition space beside the gardens.

properties.name: Ice House
properties.text: A former ice store from the days of Holland House, now a small gallery used for exhibitions.

properties.name: Iris Garden
properties.text: A planted garden near the centre of the park, at its best when the irises are in flower in early summer.

properties.name: Dutch Garden
properties.text: Formal 19th-century flower beds laid out in geometric patterns, famous for their dahlias in late summer.

properties.name: Holland Park House
properties.text: The remains of Holland House, a Jacobean mansion of 1605 largely destroyed by bombing in 1940; the surviving wing now houses a youth hostel and the terrace stages open-air opera each summer.

properties.name: Design Museum
properties.text: The Design Museum moved to its home on the southern edge of Holland Park in 2016, occupying the former Commonwealth Institute building with its distinctive hyperbolic-paraboloid roof.
